DO NOT SUBMIT. Right next move: integrate field-state INTO v4 yekenot recipe via Kaggle GPU retrain (~35 min) — bypasses rank-lock by changing the anchor itself, not stacking on top.

name: c1_yao_vehtari_path_b_K27
note: best τ=200k OOF 0.95427 vs V1 Path-B τ=100k 0.95432 = -0.47bp; all 3 τ regress; meta-arch family closed (9th variant)

Single-LGBM specialist on 18737 rain rows (INTERMEDIATE 17382 + WET 1355) gets OOF 0.92641 vs PRIMARY 0.94157 on same rows (-151.53 bp). Mixed prediction (rain=spec, dry=PRIMARY) global OOF 0.95390 vs PRIMARY 0.95432 (-4.17 bp). PRIMARY's 27-base stack extracts cross-Compound transfer signal that a rain-only specialist throws away. A19 inference (27 bases saturate the meta) re-confirmed at the segment level. Logs assumption A22 (rain-specialist axis closed) and refines A5 (residual is INTRINSIC). Open variant: rain-row sample-weighting in a global model preserves transfer; distinct from AV-sample-weighting (bounded null).

Single LGBM full-data with rain-row sample weights w in {1,3,5,10}. Even w=1 (no upweighting) gets rain AUC 0.93455 vs PRIMARY 0.94157 (-70 bp); higher weights hurt more (w=10: -102 bp). K=2 LR-meta [PRIMARY, weighted_LGBM] is uniformly null at -0.14 to -0.17 bp. PRIMARY's 27-base stack already extracts more on rain than a single LGBM can, regardless of class reweighting. A9 rank-lock confirmed at K=2 (smallest meta scale). Refines W1: rain residual is intrinsic given any single-model addition.

Compound × Stint × Year (3-way) on K=27 pool. 120 cells; 43/120 fit at MIN_ROWS=1000 (rest fall back to global). tau in {5k, 20k, 100k}: -2.25, -0.61, -0.16 bp vs PRIMARY (Compound × Stint, tau=100k). At tau=100k all alt segmentations land at near-PRIMARY OOF because heavy shrinkage to global dominates. ρ=0.99942. Combined with d14 sweep (Year/YxStint/Race × tau, 9 variants null at K=21), d18 Compound×Year (null at K=24), Yao/Vehtari covariance variant (null), per-segment-stacker family is now 11+ variants tested all at-or-below PRIMARY. Logs A9b (the rank-lock now extends to hier-meta segmentation crosses).

Compound × RaceProgress-bin (5x5 = 25 cells) on K=27. 21/25 populated. tau in {5k, 20k, 100k}: -1.71, -0.65, -0.16 bp vs PRIMARY. ρ=0.99948. Same conclusion as cs_y — alt segmentations converge to PRIMARY OOF at high tau (heavy shrinkage), and at low tau cells are too small to fit. Path-B amp at K=27 is +0.03 bp vs global LR-meta — exhausted.

metric_check_note: hinge / squared-hinge / log-loss are smooth max-margin / log-likelihood proxies; they rank well empirically (LR-bank uses log-loss too). Q6 PASS by analogy. Kernel-logistic variant (Nystroem+LR) is the calibrated cross-check per PI request.
note: First SVM-class entry on this comp (mechanism ledger empty for SVM). Three variants: RFF+SGD-hinge (cheap), Nystroem+LinearSVC (canonical), Nystroem+LR (calibrated). Pricing precedent: LR-bank ceiling 0.928, K=21+1 single-base median ~0bp / non-recipe-transfer best +0.5-1bp. Family is rank-locked — escape requires structural disagreement with GBDT in margin regions.

metric_check_note: smoke phase only; standalone OOF probe to gate full 5-fold spend
note: Smoke gate for SVM family. Cost = 1-fold @ 50k rows × 3 variants. If smoke OOF clears LR-ceiling 0.928, escalate to full 5-fold. If not, family is closed.

K=10 forward-greedy sparse pool + Path-B Compound x Stint tau=100k. Bases: d17_h1d_yekenot_full, p1_single_cb_v4_gpu, f1_hgbc_deep, d16_orig_continuous_only, b_lapsuntilpit, baseline_two_anchor, d9_R6_next_compound, cb_year-cat, e5_optuna_lgbm, d9f_FM_A. PRIMARY (K=27) buys us EXACTLY 1.2 bp on LB, matching the OOF gap to 0.1 bp precision. Confirms (A26) the bank is highly redundant at LB level - 17 of 27 bases produce 1.2 bp of LB lift collectively. Confirms (A27) OOF->LB relative transfer is precise. Falsifies user's hope that sparse pool would break the ceiling - sparse predicts at the ceiling.

GRU at K=10+1 plain LR-meta: -0.045 bp NULL (vs original -0.043 bp at K=22+1). Confirms rank-lock at LR meta is pool-size-independent. Falsifies the 'dense pool was hiding signal' framing for the GRU candidate. Strong prior to skip rerunning field-state / H9 / combined-frame lead/lag - they would absorb identically. The 4th direction must come from a structurally different inductive bias (different task framing), not from re-evaluating prior NULL bases. Logs A28.

K=4 forward-greedy (yekenot-RealMLP + p1_cb_v4 + f1_hgbc_deep + d16_orig_continuous_only) + Path-B C x Stint tau=100k. LB 0.95351 vs PRIMARY 0.95368 (Δ -1.7 bp). OOF Δ -2.93 bp - LB Δ -1.7 bp = LB *better* than OOF predicted by ~1.2 bp. Refines A27: cross-submission LB delta trust band is ~±1 bp, not ±0.1 bp. Logs A26b. K=4 captures 99% of bank's LB value with 15% of bases - 23 of 27 bases buy 1.7 bp on LB collectively. Operationally we can run with 4 bases in production with negligible LB cost.

name: svm_kernel_smoke_1e
note: Falsified at gate. linsvc K=27+1 Δ=-0.02bp / K=10+1 Δ=-0.09bp; klogreg K=27+1 Δ=-0.00bp / K=10+1 Δ=-0.06bp. Standalone OOF -400bp from PRIMARY. 8th rank-lock confirmation: kernel-class structural diversity (ρ=0.82-0.83) insufficient when standalone AUC gap to GBDT-class is too large.

Three structurally-different inductive biases on 14 raw cols all absorb at K=10+1 LR meta within +/-0.05 bp despite low Spearman rho to K=10 (0.41-0.73). EXP-2 LambdaRank (rho 0.73, Δ +0.042 bp), EXP-3 inter-stint memory (rho 0.47, Δ -0.011), EXP-4 dual-head decomposition (rho 0.41, Δ -0.014 head A / +0.035 composed). Pins down rank-lock mechanism: it's at the LOGIT-direction level, not rank-correlation. K=10 [P, rank, logit] expansion (30 features) reconstructs any new base's logit as linear combination. Different rank info doesn't add logit dimension. Logs A29, A30. Strategic implication: 3-D logit subspace is information ceiling of 14-col row-level data under LR-meta family. Breaking it requires either new data outside 14 cols (PI ruled out external) or non-LR meta architecture (gradient-boosting on predictions or neural meta-learner) - last untested avenue.

Four-variant PCA probe on K=27 ensemble. Variant A (PCA-truncate + LR) shows top-3 PCs score only 0.95061 (-35.64 bp vs K=10 anchor) - the 3.23 variance-eff-rank does NOT predict 3-D predictive ceiling; predictive content spreads across ~15 PCs. Variant B (LightGBM as meta) NULL/NEGATIVE at every input representation: top-K PCs (-1.91 to -32.88), K=10 raw expansion (-1.25), K=27 raw expansion (+0.01); CONFIRMS A30 prediction in the negative direction - non-LR meta is *worse*, not better. EXP-NEW (only architecturally untested avenue per A30) closes FALSIFIED. Variant C (Path-B C×S on PCs) NULL by huge margin (-28 to -34 bp): PCA decorrelates routing variables, killing Path-B's per-segment lift mechanism - clean falsification of the 'Path-B exploits correlation' intuition. Variant D (K=10 LR + K=27 residuals after stripping top-S PCs) recovers K=27+1 plain LR exactly (0.95428) for both S=3 and S=5: the +1 bp K=27→K=27 over K=10 lives in low-variance PCs but the LR meta on K=27 already captures it. No LB submission candidate. Strategic implications: A25 wording wants 'variance' qualifier (predictive eff-rank is closer to 15); A30 'non-LR meta could break ceiling' clause is empirically refuted; only remaining 'new direction' axis per A30 is data outside 14 cols (PI ruled out). Strengthens acceptance/wrap-up posture.
IEEE-CIS Fraud 1st-place trick: groupby(UID).transform('mean') as post-process. UID = (Driver, Race, Year). Pure post-process; no FE pipeline.

name: a3_7_uid_smoothing
note: FAIL. Groupby-mean removes the within-UID lap-to-lap variance that IS the pit-decision signal. Mechanism mismatch with IEEE Fraud (recurring-transaction UID); F1-pit rows within (Driver,Race,Year) have heterogeneous pit probabilities. Tested 4 variants (naive_pure/blend, loo_pure/blend); all regress -124 to -1709 bp. See scripts/artifacts/probe_a3_7_uid_smoothing_results.json.

RF-yekenot most-diverse positively-gating base on K=4: rho=0.959 (vs typical 0.996 for absorbed bases), K=4+1 LR-meta +0.26 bp (4.4x d15c ET-on-raw's +0.06 bp at K=22+1). Standalone OOF +12 bp over ET-on-raw 0.92967. Hedge-eligible per R5; Path-B refit on K=5 is the natural follow-up. NO LB submission yet — requires PI sign-off per Rule 1.

RF-meta over K=4 [P,rank,logit] loses 1.54 bp OOF to LR-meta. Bagged-tree variant of Day-20 PCA-meta probe finding (LightGBM-meta lost 1-2 bp). Closes non-LR meta family across boosted+bagged tree-class metas. The 3-D logit subspace ceiling is robust to inductive-class swap.

RF on combined input (K=4 expansion + 6 raw numerics) loses 0.7 bp to LR-on-same. Adding raw numerics doesn't rescue tree-class meta. Confirms `combined-input-meta-stacker-absorbed`: K=4 bases already extract all useful raw-feature signal at the base level.

name: a2_2_mandatory_compound_rule_pathb
note: Path-B amp test on K=4+A2-2 PRIMARY meta. Single-LGBM +1.4 bp absorbed mostly: K=4+1 plain LR-meta +0.30 bp; K=4+1 Path-B C×S τ=100k +0.26 bp. Path-B amp unchanged: +0.34 bp on K=4 vs +0.30 bp on K=4+1. ρ=0.999893 vs PRIMARY (TIE_EXPECTED on pre-submit diff). WEAK verdict; below +0.5 strict gate. Confirms rank-lock prediction at K=4 with Path-B. See scripts/artifacts/probe_a2_2_pathb_K4.json.

name: a2_8_stack_meta_K4
note: LightGBM stack-meta on K=4: 43 meta features (4 raw P + 4 logits + 4 ranks + 6 pairwise products + 6 abs-diffs + 6 logit-diffs + 8 numeric side-info + 5 Compound one-hot). Hyper: depth 4, leaves 31, lr 0.05, l2 1.0, bag 0.9, ff 0.9, min_data 200. 5-fold OOF 0.95390 vs Path-B PRIMARY 0.95403 (-1.30 bp); also below plain LR 0.95399 (-0.96 bp). Fold-std 0.00080 (elevated; range 0.95309-0.95506). Confirms prior EXP-NEW falsification: tree stackers overfit interaction noise on small-K pools; convex LR + Path-B partial-pooling regularize better. See scripts/artifacts/probe_a2_8_stack_meta.json.

Kitchen-sink RF on widened tableau (57 feat). Feature breadth hurts standalone OOF -1.24 bp vs yekenot-only; K=4+1 LR-meta lift unchanged within fold noise (+0.25 bp). First reproducibility check on forest-base lift across two RF feature configurations — raises prior the +0.25 bp signal is real not fold noise. Bottleneck is rank-lock at logit-direction level not feature substrate. PI hypothesis (RF scales with feature breadth per irrigation precedent) refuted on raw+engineered features (irrigation gain came from already-distilled prob vectors).

Optuna search over RF hyperparameters on 57-feat kitchen-sink. Predict null/marginal — rank-lock at logit-direction level (A30) caps meta-utility regardless of RF settings. Run because PI directed; learning value is whether the +0.25 bp signal is hyperparameter-sensitive.

15-trial TPE search + 2-seed full-5-fold validation. Best config log2/max_samples=0.7/depth=15/leaf=100/entropy. Across 4 independent RF runs (Angle A, Kitchen-sink, Optuna seed 42, Optuna seed 7) the K=4+1 lift sits in +0.24-0.27 bp with std 0.013 bp — signal is real, not fold noise; AND hyperparameter tuning yields zero meaningful improvement past the natural +0.25 bp ceiling. The lift is set by the meta architecture (3-D logit subspace ceiling, A30), not by RF. Stop tuning RF. Only remaining forest-family lever: Path-B Compound x Stint tau=100k refit on K=5.

Path-B C×S τ=100k refit on K=5 = K=4 forward-greedy + RF-yekenot (Angle A). PI approved after Optuna falsification confirmed +0.25 bp ceiling. Tests whether per-segment shrinkage transfers the OOF signal to LB. Historical 1.4× amp floor on similarly-orthogonal new bases (d15b DAE precedent: +0.715 bp OOF → +1.0 bp LB).

metric_check_note: Aggregate features added to LR meta-stacker on K=4 [P, rank, logit]; same row-level binary cross-entropy objective as PRIMARY; metric is row-AUC; no target reframing.

P(useful) 0.20 reflects 11/11 prior nulls on segment-axis variants but B is structurally novel (tree per segment exploits non-linear interactions inside Compound x Stint cells).

metric_check_note: Transformer outputs PitNextLap probability per row via sigmoid; binary cross-entropy training; row-AUC metric — aligned.
note: EXP-9 in EXPERIMENTS-NEXT.md; d16 GRU was NULL but had no gap modelling; A32 says gap-as-feature is absorbed but gap-as-positional-encoding is architecturally different. Cost 60 min CPU author+push (Kaggle GPU runs async, no local CPU spent). P(useful) 0.18 reflects A32 narrowing.

submit_decision: HELD per Rule 27 (rho>0.999 abort, LB will tie); saved as R5 hedge
note: Path-B C×S absorbs the +0.25 bp K=4+1 forest lift down to +0.02 bp vs K=4 PRIMARY. Confirms Day-15 friction path-b-amp-only-fires-on-meta-arch-not-base-add; quantifies threshold at ~+0.5 bp standalone OOF lift below which Path-B absorbs single-base orthogonal additions. Forest family characterized end-to-end: forest-as-meta dead, forest-as-base capped at +0.25 bp K=4+1, Path-B absorbs at LB. Stop the forest axis.

metric_check_note: Layer-1 stackers (LGBM/XGB/CB/MLP) all output binary-AUC probabilities; Layer-2 LR over (base + L1) OOFs is binary cross-entropy; metric is row-AUC; full alignment.
note: Replicates masayakawamata-stack architecture (LB 0.9544 from 2 base OOFs via cuML 2-layer stack). We've tested 11 per-segment-stacker variants (all LR-class L2) and 2 non-LR L2 (falsified). NEVER tested L1 STACKERS as a separate layer feeding L2 LR. Direct architectural gap. Precedent: masaya 2-base 2-layer at LB 0.9544 (+9 bp from us); ours has 4 bases so absorption ceiling argument may not bite. P(useful) 0.40 reflects the empirical existence proof on the public LB.

metric_check_note: Per-segment LGBM head replaces inner LR in Path-B; outer combiner remains LR; meta input is K=4 [P, rank, logit]; binary cross-entropy with row-AUC metric.
note: W5 third bullet untested (non-LR per-segment head in Path-B). 11 prior per-segment variants all LR-class. Cheap CPU-only falsification. P(useful) 0.20 reflects 11/11 prior LR-class nulls but B is structurally novel (tree per-segment exploits non-linear interactions). Cost 90 min revised down from 120 (parallel with Prong S).

name: seq_coupled_v1_lr_full
note: V1.2 LR meta on K=4 [P,rank,logit]+24 seq-coupled features (36 feat) 5-fold OOF AUC 0.95401 vs LR row-local 12-feat 0.95400 (+0.03 bp WEAK). Diagnostic confirmed the 24 seq-coupled features have R²=0.487 vs row-local span — 51% non-overlap variance — yet LR meta extracts +0.03 bp. Refines A29 framing: rank-lock is at the conditional-target-correlation level, not just the logit-direction level. See scripts/seq_coupled/diag_lookahead_orthogonality.py + fit_meta.py + audit/2026-05-08-seq-coupled-meta-probe.md.

name: seq_coupled_v2_interactions_residual_lgbm
note: V2 sharpenings on 36-feat input. V2.1 LR + hand interactions (47 feat): OOF 0.95400 (-0.30 bp vs PRIMARY) TIE. V2.2 stage-1 LR row-local + stage-2 Ridge on residuals: OOF 0.95369 (-3.43 bp REGRESS) — Ridge fits noise. V2.3 LightGBM meta on 36-feat: OOF 0.95397 (-0.59 bp REGRESS). Confirms tree-stack-meta-overfits-small-K-pool friction even with sequence-coupled feature enrichment. No conditional structure breaks the rank-lock at K=4.

name: seq_coupled_v1_ranknet
note: V1.3 RankNet pairwise meta on 36-feat (K=4 row-local + 24 seq-coupled): 5-fold OOF AUC 0.95399 vs LR row-local 0.95400 (-0.12 bp). RankNet uses direct AUC objective via pairwise sigmoid loss on 2M pos/neg pairs/fold × 200 epochs × momentum SGD. Per-fold AUCs match LR's row-local within <0.1 bp on every fold. The ceiling is information, not optimisation. Refines structural finding: enriching meta input with sequence-coupled features adds +0.03 bp under cross-entropy and -0.12 bp under direct AUC; loss-function variation does not escape the conditional-target-correlation ceiling at K=4. probe_seq_coupled_meta.json final summary.

name: seq_coupled_v3_knn_meta
note: V3 LR meta on K=4 [P,rank,logit] + kNN target-mean + discrepancy (14 feat). 5-fold OOF AUC 0.95401 vs LR row-local 0.95400 (+0.01 bp). Per-fold: 0.95496/0.95311/0.95371/0.95339/0.95503 — all within ±0.03 bp of LR row-local. Bare y_knn AUC 0.8979 standalone (substantial signal!), discrepancy correlation with y = -0.357 (real). YET the LR meta extracts +0.01 bp from these features. Confirms K=4 conditional-target-correlation ceiling EVEN under transductive label-derived features at the meta layer. The path forward narrows to (a) external data, (b) genuinely new base class with new source information. V4 (kNN as base input) tests whether non-linear tree ingestion lifts where linear meta absorption blocks.

name: seq_coupled_v4_knn_aug_base
note: V4 kNN-augmented LightGBM base: 5-fold OOF standalone AUC 0.94163. K=4+1 plain LR-meta gate +0.24 bp on OOF (5/5 folds positive, range +0.12 to +0.44 bp). FIRST base in 8 days to produce a consistently positive K=4+1 lift, but BELOW +0.5 bp PASS gate (WEAK verdict). ρ_spearman vs K=4 bases 0.85-0.94, most diverse vs d16_orig (0.85). Tree-split ingestion of kNN-target-mean extracts +0.24 bp at meta, where the same feature at META layer in V3 extracted only +0.01 bp — confirms tree non-linearity DOES retrieve some signal LR misses, but magnitude is small.

name: seq_coupled_K5_path_b
note: Path-B Compound×Stint τ=100k production gate. K=4 PRIMARY OOF re-derived at 0.95403 (matches A1). K=5 (K=4+V4) Path-B OOF 0.95405 (Δ +0.20 bp). ρ_test_K5_vs_K4 = 0.99989 — exceeds Rule 27 abort threshold (0.999). LB expected to tie. ABORT submission per Rule 27. Submission file built but NOT pushed: submissions/submission_K5_kNNaugbase_pathb.csv. Held for potential R5 hedge in final-window if PI reviews.

name: submit_K5_kNNaugbase_pathb
note: K=5 (K=4 + V4 kNN-augmented base) + Path-B C×S τ=100k. Public LB 0.95359 vs K=4 PRIMARY 0.95351 = +0.8 bp. OOF was +0.20 bp; LB amplification ~4x (partly sample noise; partly real V4 contribution). Rule 27 abort guidance (ρ_test 0.99989 > 0.999 => tie) was empirically wrong here; LB moved +0.8 bp. Calibration: ρ in 0.999-0.9999 zone can still produce sub-bp to few-bp LB movement, not auto-tie. K=27 d18 PRIMARY (0.95368) remains LB best by 9 bp; V4 didn't bridge that gap. Submitted by PI explicit Rule 27 override 2026-05-09.

name: seq_coupled_v5_te_aug_base
note:
V5 = V4 (kNN-target-mean) + 5 fold-safe multi-cell target encodings (Compound×Stint, ×Stint×RaceProgress-bin, Driver×Compound, Driver×Stint, Race×Compound; alpha=50 smoothing). Standalone OOF AUC 0.94224 vs V4 0.94163 = +6.10 bp at base level (5/5 folds positive). At META layer:
  K=4+V5 LR meta:   +0.07 bp vs K=4 (much LESS than V4's +0.24)
  K=6 (K=4+V4+V5): +0.06 bp vs current K=5 PRIMARY
ρ_spearman V5 vs V4 = 0.989 — V5 is in V4's logit direction. TE features add signal kNN-target-mean already captures; redundant at meta. No production swap. K=5 PRIMARY stands. Mechanism takeaway: V4 captured most of the transductive lift available at K=4; V5 confirmed by absorption.

name: seq_coupled_v6_embedding_knn_base
note:
V6 = task-learned-embedding kNN base. Per fold: train MLP (64→32→16 ReLU; max_iter=60, early-stopping) on training rows in folds≠k → extract 16-dim penultimate embedding for all rows → BallTree kNN(K=20) in embedding space → use kNN-target-mean alongside V4-style raw-feature kNN-target-mean as inputs to a LightGBM base. MLP per-fold AUC ~0.908. Standalone OOF AUC 0.94200 vs V4 0.94163 = +3.75 bp at base level (5/5 folds positive).
At META layer (K=4+1 LR meta gate):
  K=4+V6 (V6 replaces V4):  +0.18 bp vs K=4 (LESS than V4's +0.24)
  K=6 (K=4+V4+V6):          -0.02 bp vs K=5 PRIMARY plain LR
ρ_spearman(V6, V4) = 0.988 — same pattern as V5 (ρ 0.989). Embedding-kNN and raw-feature-kNN produce essentially the same logit direction at the meta despite different similarity metrics. Transductive-feature mechanism is fundamentally one-dimensional with respect to the K=4 logit direction. V-series saturated; V4 (+0.24 bp meta / +0.80 bp LB) is the high-water mark.

name: r11_C_survival_hazard
note: Cox-PH at TyreLife with per-stint covariate aggregates as base. Standalone 0.65 G1-FAIL (row-level signal collapses onto baseline h0(t)). K=14+Path-B DCS tau=100k Delta=-0.049 bp NULL, rho_OOF 0.99992 TIE_ZONE. Mechanism C CLOSED at this formulation. Same shape as R9 NB4 (-0.022) and R9 C1 (-0.045).

name: r11_A_transformer_skip
note: R10 priority queue A=seq2seq transformer dedup-checked against mechanism-ledger: R5 transformer v1 (std 0.91974 absorbed at K=11+Path-B) and R6 transformer v2 (K=14+Path-B Delta=-0.014 bp absorbed) both already CLOSED per HANDOVER line 134. 'predict next-N PitNextLap' framing is a head-swap not a structural pivot. PI directive: skip A; redirect ~2h Kaggle T4 budget to B.

name: r11_B_graph_transverse
note: Transverse cross-driver attention transformer (Kaggle T4x2). Hit MAX_DRIVERS=24 truncation BUG: 80% of (Year, Race, LapNumber) groups exceed 24 drivers (median 58, mean 71, max 373 — synthetic data has 887 unique pseudo-drivers vs ~22 real F1). 70% of OOF entries left at 0.0. Standalone 0.62 G1-FAIL. K=14+Path-B DCS tau=100k Delta=-0.035 bp NULL despite the bug — base mostly noise via Path-B shrinkage. Even at MAX_DRIVERS=128 (covers ~85% of groups) the inter-driver-competition signal is structurally degraded: 71+ pseudo-drivers per race-lap dissolves F1 cohort cohesion. Mechanism B CLOSED at this formulation; richer formulation would need to model the data's actual augmentation structure, not real-F1 dynamics.

name: r12_cb_resid
note: CatBoost-regression on residual target (y - oof_R7.1). Plan-agent's highest-EV orthogonality lever. Per-fold ES fires at iter 1-15 because val RMSE does not improve from epoch 0 -- the residual has no exploitable conditional structure given x. Standalone AUC 0.478 (BELOW random) confirms: R7.1+Path-B is at the Bayes calibration ceiling on this dataset. K=14 add Delta=-0.0701 bp NULL. This is a STRONG noise-ceiling confirmation, not a model-quality issue: a perfectly-tuned CatBoost cannot extract signal that does not exist. Implication: residual-target probes are all closed; same applies to ANY mechanism trying to predict the residual of an already-saturated meta.

name: r12_cb_horizon
note: CatBoost regression on log(LapsUntilPit+1) with STRICT PER-FOLD target derivation (no Day-15 leakage; verified standalone proxy AUC 0.88 is genuine). FIRST positive K=14 add since R7.1 was set: Delta=+0.046 bp OOF vs R7.1 PRIMARY 0.954471 -> K=14 0.954475. Below the +0.10 bp G2 submission threshold but rho_test=0.999828 (OK transfer band, just below TIE_ZONE 0.9999). Base column rho vs R7.1=0.626 / vs cb_v4=0.607 -- genuinely orthogonal direction. Different target carries multi-step future info that K=13's single-step predictors lack -- mechanism-class novel. Plan-agent BOTE was P(G2)=0.25 / E[Delta]=+0.10 bp; outcome below that but positive. Submission decision deferred to PI.

name: submit_r12_cb_horizon_K14
note: R12-2 cb_horizon K=14 + Path-B DCS tau=100k. LB 0.95392 = NEW PRIMARY (+0.03 bp vs R7.1 0.95389). OOF Delta was +0.0460 bp (sub-G2 +0.10 bp threshold) but rho_test OK-band correctly predicted sub-bp to few-bp LB movement; actual +0.03 bp lands inside that band. FIRST LB improvement in 18 days (R7.1 set 2026-05-18). Mechanism: CatBoost regression on log(LapsUntilPit+1) provides multi-step-future info that K=13's single-step PitNextLap predictors lack -- different target -> different gradient signal -> structurally novel base, rho_base vs R7.1 0.626 -- the orthogonality hypothesis from the plan-agent pressure-test PAID OFF on the second variant after cb_resid confirmed the Bayes ceiling. Slot used: 2/10 today (R10 HEDGE 3 earlier; R12-2 just now). PRIMARY now: R12 (K=14 = K=13 + cb_horizon) + Path-B DriverClass x Stint tau=100k.

name: r13_B_operator_sweep
note: Operator-class diagnostic confirms cb_horizon adds at LR-meta across C in {0.1, 1.0, 10.0} (+0.02 to +0.16 bp). Path-B segmentation adds an additional +0.15 bp on top of best LR-meta. Both axes contribute: base diversity (cb_horizon) AND meta-segmentation (Path-B DCS). Implication: scaling BASE diversity (more orthogonal-target CBs as K=15+) is the higher-EV move; replacing Path-B with global xendcg-meta likely regresses by losing the +0.15 bp segmentation benefit. Reorder swing: prioritize cb_stint_completion (K=15 base add) over Phase C xendcg-meta-replacement.

name: r13_cb_stint_completion
note: R13 Phase D: CatBoost regression on stint-completion fraction (TyreLife / stint_max within (Driver, Race, Stint) STRICT per fold). Standalone AUC 0.63 (moderate); rank-normalized base column rho vs R12-2 = 0.0006 -- near-PERFECT orthogonality, an even cleaner orthogonal direction than cb_horizon (rho 0.626). K=15 (K=13 + cb_horizon + cb_stint_completion) + Path-B DCS tau=100k: OOF Delta +0.0934 bp vs R12-2 (just below strict G2 +0.10 but cleanly above the calibrated +0.02 floor); rho_test 0.99993 OK band. Two orthogonal-target CBs successfully STACK -- the swing's central thesis (base diversity in different target-class scales) is now empirically confirmed for two variants.

name: submit_r13_K15
note: Submitted R13 K=15 = K=13 + cb_horizon + cb_stint_completion + Path-B DCS tau=100k. Pre-submit: rho_test 0.99993 OK band. Slot 3/10 today (R10 HEDGE 3 + R12-2 cb_horizon + R13 K=15). Slot budget: hold remaining 7 for future days (plan-agent rec ≥5 reserved). LB result pending.

name: submit_r13_K15_LB
note: R13 K=15 = K=13 + cb_horizon + cb_stint_completion + Path-B DCS tau=100k. LB 0.95393 = NEW PRIMARY (+0.01 bp vs R12-2 0.95392). Just at the 1-quantization-step public LB noise floor. Cumulative session lift: R7.1 0.95389 -> R12-2 0.95392 (+0.03) -> R13 0.95393 (+0.01) = total +0.04 bp today across 2 orthogonal-target CBs. Top-5% gap closed from -1.6 bp to -1.2 bp (+0.4 bp closed). Slot 3/10 used today; per plan ≤3 cap reached. Plan-agent thesis (scale base diversity at K=N+) confirmed for second consecutive variant.

name: r14_phase1_pathb_seg_sweep
note: R14 Phase 1: Path-B segmentation sweep on K=15 pool (R13 + cb_horizon + cb_stint_completion). 7 segs tested at tau=100k. Survivors (Delta vs R5.2 ≥ +0.10 bp): driverclass_stint (+0.245), driver_tier_stint (+0.168), compound_stint_lapbucket (+0.155), race_cluster_stint (+0.150). vs R13 PRIMARY (0.954485): ALL alt-segs regressed (best alternative driver_tier_stint at -0.078 bp). DCS tau=100k confirmed as optimal segmentation on K=15. NO submission from this phase.

name: r14_cb_first_pit_lap
note: R14 Phase 5 promoted (replaced cb_next_compound which smoke-failed AUC 0.36 anti-correlated). CatBoost regression on log1p(absolute first-pit lap in race) per (Driver, Race). Strict per-fold target. Standalone OOF AUC 0.535 (barely above random; per-fold ES at iter 4 because target is group-uniform and weakly correlated with row features). K=16 (R13 pool + cb_first_pit_lap) + Path-B DCS tau=100k Delta vs R13 = -0.0206 bp NULL. Below calibrated gate. NO submission.

name: r14_cb_next_compound_dropped
note: R14 Phase 3 cb_next_compound (5-class CatBoost on next-stint Compound, P(change) as rank signal). Smoke AUC 0.36 anti-correlated with PitNextLap. Dropped before full run; replaced with cb_first_pit_lap (Phase 5 promoted).

name: r14_tabm
note: R14 Phase 2: TabM (pytabkit TabM_D_Classifier) full 5-fold on Kaggle T4x2. NN-class diversity per brainstorm S2 (+0.3-0.8 bp predicted standalone). Standalone OOF AUC 0.93856 -- strong base (in K=13 pool's middle range; cb_horizon range). Base orthogonality EXCELLENT: rho vs R13 PRIMARY=0.926 (much more orthogonal than cb_horizon's 0.626), rho vs cb_stint_completion=0.127. K=16 (R13 pool + TabM) + Path-B DCS tau=100k: OOF Delta vs R13 = +0.0193 bp. JUST BELOW the calibrated +0.02 gate floor (by 0.001 bp -- effectively at noise threshold). rho_test 0.99991 = OK band edge. tau-sweep (20k/50k/100k/200k) confirms tau=100k optimal; 20k -0.255, 50k -0.039, 200k -0.009 bp NULL. PRIMARY R13 unchanged today. SUBMISSION DECISION DEFERRED to PI for Day 20 window; cumulative R14 result: 4 of 4 phases failed to clear gate by clear margin -- orthogonal-target CB axis is saturating; TabM's NN-class diversity didn't break the geometric-decline pattern beyond noise floor.

name: r14_tabm_tau_sweep
note: Path-B tau sweep on K=16+TabM: tau=100k remains optimal. tau=20k catastrophic regression (-0.255 bp) due to over-shrinkage of TabM base. tau=200k under-shrinks (-0.009 bp). The +0.019 bp at tau=100k is the achievable ceiling for this stack with this segmentation.

name: submit_r14_tabm_K16_LB
note: R14 K=16 (R13 + TabM) + Path-B DCS tau=100k. LB 0.95395 = NEW PRIMARY (+0.02 bp vs R13 0.95393). TabM at +0.019 bp OOF (below the +0.02 gate by 0.001 bp) registered +0.02 bp LB -- the OK-band lower-edge rho_test 0.99991 was MORE forgiving on LB than the predicted noise-floor would suggest. Cumulative session gains: R7.1 0.95389 -> R12-2 0.95392 -> R13 0.95393 -> R14 0.95395 = +0.6 bp closed against top-5% boundary 0.95405. Gap now -1.0 bp (was -1.6 bp). FOURTH PRIMARY swap in one session via four distinct orthogonal mechanisms (count-regression, fraction-regression, NN-class) stacked into Path-B K=16. Calibration data point: sub-G2 OOF (+0.019 bp) + OK-band edge rho (0.99991) -> +0.02 bp LB. Confirms gate-floor-+0.02 threshold is correctly calibrated -- borderline candidates near the floor DO register on LB.

name: r15_phase1_xendcg_per_seg
note: R15 Phase 1: per-segment xendcg-meta as META replacement (replaces per-segment LR-meta + Path-B shrinkage). Without Path-B's cross-segment shrinkage, per-segment xendcg OVERFITS each segment independently -- OOF AUC 0.890 vs R14 PRIMARY 0.954 (-639 bp regression). Path-B shrinkage is doing FAR more work than the +0.15 bp R13 Phase B diagnostic surfaced (which was the LR-meta-global vs LR-meta-segmented-with-shrinkage delta; the ABSOLUTE shrinkage contribution to per-segment is 60+ bp). PIVOT: treat the xendcg-meta output as a NEW 17th BASE column (rho_OOF vs R14 = 0.643 suggests some orthogonal signal); K=17 = R14 K=16 + xendcg-as-base via Path-B LR-meta. Running.
